Notice Of Motion And Motion To To Compel Attendance At Deposition And Testimony Of Qualified Witnesses;Production Of Documents At Deposition;Answers To Questions The Witness Was Instructed Not To Answer By Counsel
Off Calendar Nov-03-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Colin T. Kemp, Esq., for Judity Witcomb, Defendant/Moving Party
David S. Secrest, Esq., for Lynde Christensen, Plaintiff/Opposing party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintif Stephen Williams' Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition and Testimony of Qualified Witnesses, Production Of Documents At Deposition, and Answers To Questions The Witness Was Instructed Not To Answer By Counsel is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to deposition topic No. One to the Person Most Knowledgeable. As those documents previously have been produced, it is appropriate to identify the previously identified documents without making a new production.
The motion is granted as to deposition topic Number 2 to the Person Most Knowledgeable. As those documents previously have been produced, it is appropriate to identify the previously identified documents without making a new production.
The motion is granted as to deposition topic Number 5.
The motion is denied as to deposition topic Numbers 7, 8, 9, and 10.
As to the questions in dispute as to the deposition of Moomau, the objections to any response except as it relates to sexual orientation discrimination and harassment is sustained.
The Person Most Knowledgeable shall appear for further deposition with production of documents. Production or identification of responsive documents shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Marilyn Moomau shall appear for further deposition if Plaintiff wishes to obtain responses as limited by this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
